title: eupatorium guadalupense var. laxum
intro: Eupatorium guadalupense var. laxum, also known as Mexican Joe Pye Weed, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It is a member of the sunflower family and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever and relieving symptoms of colds and flu, as well as providing relief from muscle and joint pain. Eupatorium guadalupense var. laxum can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
